Test Plan Document - Main Points
1. Test Plan Identifier
•	Unique ID or version of the test plan.
2. Introduction
•	Purpose and scope of testing.
3. Test Items
•	Modules, features, or applications to be tested.
4. Features to be Tested
•	Functionalities included in testing.
5. Features Not to be Tested
•	Items excluded from testing.
6. Test Approach / Strategy
•	Functional Testing
•	Integration Testing
•	System Testing
•	Regression Testing
•	API Testing
•	UI Testing
•	Manual and Automation approach
7. Entry Criteria
•	Conditions that must be met before testing starts.
8. Exit Criteria
•	Conditions that must be satisfied to complete testing.
9. Test Environment
•	Hardware requirements
•	Software requirements
•	Browsers and operating systems
•	Database details
•	Testing tools
10. Test Data
•	Data required for executing test cases.
11. Roles and Responsibilities
•	QA Lead
•	Test Engineers
•	Developers
•	Business Analysts
•	Project Managers
12. Test Deliverables
•	Test Cases
•	Requirement Traceability Matrix (RTM)
•	Defect Reports
•	Test Execution Reports
•	Test Summary Reports
13. Defect Management Process
•	Defect lifecycle
•	Severity and Priority definitions
14. Schedule and Milestones
•	Test planning timeline
•	Test execution schedule
•	Release milestones
15. Risks and Mitigation
•	Potential risks
•	Impact analysis
•	Mitigation plans
16. Assumptions and Dependencies
•	External dependencies
•	Assumptions affecting testing
17. Approvals
•	Stakeholder sign-off
•	Management approval
________________________________________
Summary
The key sections of a Test Plan document are:
•	Scope and Objectives
•	Features to be Tested and Not Tested
•	Test Strategy
•	Entry and Exit Criteria
•	Test Environment
•	Test Data
•	Roles and Responsibilities
•	Test Deliverables
•	Schedule and Milestones
•	Risks and Mitigation
•	Assumptions and Dependencies
•	Approvals
